_comment: WORKED EXAMPLE derived from the real, top-scoring in-place ARA: DreamerV3 (Mastering Diverse Domains through World Models, arXiv 2301.04104) — Seal-2 grade Strong Accept (mean 4.67), the best of the落位 ai_package set. Shown to teach the EXACT shape resolve_analysis must return, using real content. Numbers are the paper's own. Delete the _comment key in real output (the engine ignores it). See ../examples/worked-example.md for the end-to-end walkthrough and ../references/ara-schema.md for the field contract.
overview: DreamerV3 is a general-purpose model-based RL algorithm that learns a world model of the environment and optimizes an actor-critic policy entirely inside imagined latent trajectories. With a single fixed hyperparameter configuration it matches or beats domain-specialized expert algorithms across more than 150 diverse tasks. Its core contribution is a set of robustness techniques (symlog transforms, KL free bits, percentile return normalization, symexp twohot loss) that remove the cross-domain training instability caused by signal-magnitude differences.

observations.statement: Reward magnitudes, observation scales, and exploration needs differ by orders of magnitude across domains (continuous vs discrete actions, vision vs low-dim, dense vs sparse reward).
observations.evidence: Specialized RL methods are tuned per-domain and do not transfer their hyperparameters.
observations.implication: Applying RL to a new domain requires expert manual tuning, which bottlenecks adoption.

gaps.statement: No single fixed configuration reaches competitive performance simultaneously across continuous control, discrete games, sparse reward, and 3D open worlds.
gaps.why_fail: Cross-domain signal-magnitude differences make any one configuration break on some domain.

insight.statement: Compressing signal magnitude (symlog), stabilizing representations (KL free bits), and normalizing returns by percentiles together eliminate cross-domain signal differences, so one fixed configuration learns everywhere; meanwhile the world model's unsupervised reconstruction objective itself yields rich representations.
insight.enables: Tuning-free learning across more than 150 tasks with a single configuration.

assumptions: A reward and episode-continuation signal are available during training.
The discrete latent dynamics are approximately Markov given the recurrent state.

claims.title: Fixed-config cross-domain mastery
claims.statement: DreamerV3 uses one fixed hyperparameter configuration to match or surpass specialized expert algorithms across more than 150 diverse tasks spanning 8 domains, and far exceeds general PPO.
claims.falsification: Find a domain where fixed-config DreamerV3 is systematically worse than the tuned expert baseline.
claims.interpretation: A single robust recipe replaces per-domain hyperparameter engineering.

claims.title: Minecraft diamonds from scratch
claims.statement: DreamerV3 is the first algorithm to collect a diamond in Minecraft from scratch with no human data and no curriculum, reaching a 9.1 episode return at 100M environment steps versus 7.1 for the strongest baseline.
claims.falsification: Show a prior algorithm collected diamonds under identical from-scratch conditions.
claims.interpretation: World-model imagination makes long-horizon sparse-reward exploration tractable without human priors.

concepts.name: Recurrent State-Space Model (RSSM)
concepts.definition: A world model with a deterministic recurrent state h_t and a stochastic discrete latent z_t, factored into a sequence model, an encoder, and a dynamics predictor.
concepts.notation: h_t = f(h_{t-1}, z_{t-1}, a_{t-1}),  z_t ~ q(z_t | h_t, x_t),  \hat z_t ~ p(\hat z_t | h_t)
concepts.interpretation: A compressed simulator you can roll forward without touching real observations.
concepts.boundary: Open-loop rollouts stay reliable only within the horizon the dynamics were trained for.

concepts.name: symlog / symexp transform
concepts.definition: A sign-preserving logarithmic squashing of arbitrary-magnitude targets and its inverse.
concepts.interpretation: A volume knob that puts very large and very small targets on one comparable scale.
concepts.boundary: Distorts linear magnitude information, so it is used on targets, not on raw features that must stay linear.

concepts.name: symexp twohot loss
concepts.definition: Distributional regression over exponentially-spaced bins, training a softmax classifier whose expected bin value reconstructs the target.
concepts.notation: \hat y = softmax(f(x))^T B,  L = -twohot(y)^T log softmax(f(x))
concepts.interpretation: Predict a value by voting across log-spaced buckets, decoupling gradient scale from target magnitude.
concepts.boundary: Applied only to the reward predictor and critic, not to every regression target.

concepts.name: KL balancing and free bits
concepts.definition: Separate dynamics and representation KL terms, each clipped at a small floor so optimization stops once the KL is small enough.
concepts.notation: L_dyn = max(1, KL[sg(q) || p]),  L_rep = max(1, KL[q || sg(p)])
concepts.interpretation: Stop pushing the KL once it is small to avoid degenerate zero-information latents and KL spikes.
concepts.boundary: The free-bits floor must be tuned in nats; too high a floor leaves the prior under-trained.

concepts.name: imagination training
concepts.definition: Training the actor and critic entirely on open-loop latent rollouts started from replayed model states, with no real-environment interaction during policy learning.
concepts.interpretation: The policy practices inside the learned dream rather than the real world, and uses no lookahead search.
concepts.boundary: Quality is bounded by the world model's fidelity over the rollout horizon.

experiments.title: Fixed-config cross-domain benchmark

setup.model: DreamerV3 at the default model size with one fixed configuration
setup.dataset: 8 benchmark domains covering 150+ tasks (Atari, ProcGen, DMLab, Atari100K, BSuite, proprio and visual continuous control)
setup.system: Each domain run under its standard protocol; DreamerV3 never re-tuned

procedure: Train DreamerV3 with identical hyperparameters on every domain.
Compare against fixed-HP PPO and per-domain tuned expert algorithms.
experiments.expected: At least matches tuned experts on most domains and far exceeds fixed-HP PPO.

experiments.title: Minecraft diamond from scratch

setup.model: DreamerV3 out-of-the-box at the default model size
setup.hardware: 1x A100 plus 64 parallel CPU env workers
setup.dataset: MineRL Diamond environment, sparse reward, 12 milestone items
setup.system: From-scratch training with no human data and no curriculum

procedure: Train on sparse reward only within a 100M environment-step budget across 10 seeds.
Report episode return and the fraction of runs that collect a diamond.
experiments.expected: DreamerV3 collects diamonds while tuned baselines collect none.

experiments.title: Robustness-technique ablation

setup.model: DreamerV3 with one robustness technique removed at a time

procedure: Remove one of {KL free bits, return normalization, symexp twohot, observation symlog, unimix, critic EMA}.
Measure the change in the cross-domain mean.
experiments.expected: Every technique contributes positively to the mean, with the KL objective contributing most.

related_work.what_changed: Keeps latent imagination training but adds magnitude-robust losses so one configuration works across all domains.
related_work.why: Earlier Dreamers still needed per-environment tuning of the representation-loss weight.

related_work.what_changed: Removes the dependence on human demonstrations and hand-designed curricula for long-horizon sparse-reward play.
related_work.why: Prior Minecraft agents needed large human-play datasets or staged curricula to reach diamonds.

architecture: World Model (RSSM) -> Imagination Rollout -> Actor-Critic -> Policy. A CNN/MLP encoder maps the observation to a discrete stochastic latent z; a block-diagonal GRU sequence model carries the recurrent state h; reward, continue, and decoder heads read the model state {h, z}; the actor and critic train on H-step imagined latent rollouts, and at inference the actor samples actions with no forward search.
algorithm:
World-model loss (Eq. 2-3; weights beta_pred=1, beta_dyn=1, beta_rep=0.1):
$$ \mathcal{L}(\phi) \doteq \mathrm{E}_{q_\phi}\!\Big[ \sum_{t=1}^{T} \big( \beta_{\mathrm{pred}}\,\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{pred}}(\phi) + \beta_{\mathrm{dyn}}\,\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{dyn}}(\phi) + \beta_{\mathrm{rep}}\,\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{rep}}(\phi) \big) \Big] $$
KL terms with free bits (clip at 1 nat):
$$ \mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{dyn}}(\phi) \doteq \max\!\big(1,\, \mathrm{KL}[\mathrm{sg}(q_\phi(z_t\mid h_t,x_t)) \,\|\, p_\phi(z_t\mid h_t)]\big),\quad \mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{rep}}(\phi) \doteq \max\!\big(1,\, \mathrm{KL}[q_\phi(z_t\mid h_t,x_t) \,\|\, \mathrm{sg}(p_\phi(z_t\mid h_t))]\big) $$
Critic loss on lambda-returns (Eq. 5; gamma=0.997, lambda=0.95):
$$ \mathcal{L}(\psi) \doteq -\sum_{t=1}^{T} \ln p_\psi(R_t^\lambda \mid s_t),\quad R_t^\lambda \doteq r_t + \gamma c_t\big((1-\lambda) v_t + \lambda R_{t+1}^\lambda\big) $$
constraints:
# Constraints

- No systematic exploration mechanism: near-random on active-exploration tasks; diamonds are collected in only a small fraction of Minecraft episodes.
- One world model is trained per environment; cross-task or cross-domain sharing is left as future work.
- The actor uses a REINFORCE gradient estimator for both discrete and continuous actions, giving higher policy-gradient variance.
- Stride-2 convolutions compress images to a small spatial grid, losing precision on detail-sensitive tasks.

heuristics.desc: Clip the dynamics and representation KL terms with free bits so optimization stops once the KL is already small.
heuristics.rationale: Prevents degenerate zero-information latents and removes the KL spikes seen in deep variational models; the ablation shows removing the KL objective is the single largest hit to performance.
heuristics.sensitivity: High — too high a free-bits floor leaves the prior under-trained.

heuristics.desc: Scale the actor gradient by a percentile range of returns with a denominator floor.
heuristics.rationale: Under sparse reward the return standard deviation collapses and naive normalization amplifies noise; percentiles are outlier-robust and the floor stops tiny returns from being blown up.
heuristics.sensitivity: High — without the floor, sparse-reward exploration stalls.

configs_training.name: optimizer
configs_training.rationale: Stable across domains with adaptive gradient clipping.

configs_training.name: lr
configs_training.rationale: One learning rate shared across all domains.

configs_training.name: batch_size
configs_training.rationale: Sequences of length 64 per batch element.

configs_training.name: imagination_horizon
configs_training.rationale: Horizon over which imagined rollouts stay reliable.

configs_model.name: recurrent_units
configs_model.rationale: Block-diagonal GRU width at the default model size.

configs_model.name: model_width
configs_model.rationale: Hidden width d of the dynamics and head MLPs at the default size.

configs_model.name: params_million
configs_model.rationale: Total trainable parameters at the default (200M) model size; the family scales from 12M to 400M.

environment.framework: Unspecified in the paper (a public implementation exists)
environment.hardware: Single Nvidia A100 GPU per agent; Minecraft adds 64 CPU env workers

execution_stub:
def imagine(state, actor, world_model, horizon=15):
    h, z = state
    traj = []
    for _ in range(horizon):
        a = actor(h, z)
        h = world_model.sequence(h, z, a)
        z = world_model.dynamics(h)
        traj.append((h, z, a))
    return traj

innovations.name: Magnitude-robust losses (symlog, symexp twohot, KL free bits, percentile return normalization) for fixed-config cross-domain learning.
First from-scratch Minecraft diamond collection with no human data or curriculum.

exploration_tree.title: Why does one fixed configuration break across domains?
exploration_tree.description: Diagnose what makes a single hyperparameter set fail when reward and observation magnitudes vary by orders of magnitude.

exploration_tree.title: Choose how to handle arbitrary-magnitude targets
exploration_tree.choice: symlog squashing plus symexp twohot regression

exploration_tree.title: Plain MSE on raw-magnitude targets
exploration_tree.hypothesis: Regressing directly on raw reward and value magnitudes is sufficient.
exploration_tree.failure_mode: Large targets explode gradients while clipped losses stall at the extremes.
exploration_tree.lesson: Use a sign-preserving two-way compression rather than non-stationary running statistics.

exploration_tree.title: Robustness-technique ablation
exploration_tree.result: Each technique contributes positively to the cross-domain mean, with the KL objective contributing most.

exploration_tree.title: Choose how to normalize returns for the actor
exploration_tree.choice: percentile return range with a denominator floor

exploration_tree.title: Mean/std advantage normalization under sparse reward
exploration_tree.hypothesis: Standard advantage normalization handles sparse reward.
exploration_tree.failure_mode: When the return standard deviation collapses, normalization amplifies tiny noise and drowns the entropy regularizer.
exploration_tree.lesson: Normalize by an outlier-robust percentile range and floor the denominator.

exploration_tree.title: Minecraft diamond from scratch
exploration_tree.result: All DreamerV3 seeds eventually collect a diamond within the step budget while tuned baselines collect none.

exploration_tree.title: From per-domain tuning to a single fixed recipe
exploration_tree.to: one magnitude-robust configuration for all domains
exploration_tree.trigger: Magnitude-robust losses removed the need to re-tune the representation-loss weight per environment.

evidence_tables.name: minecraft_diamond
evidence_tables.caption: Episode return on Minecraft Diamond at 100M environment steps.

math_intuition: 把它想成一个会自动调节的音量旋钮:无论目标信号极大还是极小,都被对称地压到同一个可比较的刻度上,于是同一套损失与同一个学习率就能跨任务通用,而不必为每个领域单独调参。
math_toy_example: 把一个很大的目标值和一个很小的目标值同时丢进 symlog,二者的差距会被对称地压缩;模型于是既不会为大信号分配过多梯度,也不会忽略小信号,跨任务训练因此更平稳。

loss_highlight.direction: 把奖励与价值这类幅度差异巨大的目标,统一压到对称对数刻度上,让单一配置能跨任务稳定训练。
loss_highlight.mechanism: twohot 软分类把价值回归变成在对数刻度的若干箱子上投票,使梯度幅度与目标原始尺度解耦(链 数学方法 推导)。
loss_highlight.baseline: 相较直接对原始幅度做回归的做法,这套变换让训练在稀疏与稠密信号下都不易发散。

trend: 模型驱动的强化学习正从每个领域手工调参的专才,转向单一固定配置的通才;本文把世界模型方法推到了这一方向的前沿。
